name: Farmers And Merchants Bank Of Ashland
description_short: Family-owned community bank in Ashland, Nebraska, serving personal, business, and agricultural customers since 1883 with FDIC insurance and local decision-making.
description_long: Farmers and Merchants Bank of Ashland is a community bank established in 1883, making it over 130 years old. The bank operates as a locally-owned institution positioned between Lincoln and Omaha, Nebraska, and has maintained family ownership throughout its history. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection for customers. The bank offers three primary service lines: Personal Banking, Business Banking, and Agricultural Banking. Personal banking services include checking accounts, savings accounts, mortgage loans, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRAs, credit cards, and personal loans. Business customers have access to deposit products and loan products tailored to commercial needs. Agricultural customers receive specialized agriculture loans. All customer segments can utilize online banking, mobile apps for both Android and Apple, and fee-free ATMs. The bank also provides financial calculators for mortgages, savings, CDs, auto loans, amortization schedules, and retirement planning. What distinguishes Farmers and Merchants Bank is its emphasis on "growing roots not branches"—maintaining local ownership and decision-making rather than expanding as a chain. The bank explicitly markets itself as a family-owned institution with a "warm family atmosphere" and a 130-year commitment to community care. Their focus on three distinct banking segments (personal, business, agricultural) reflects the regional economy of rural Nebraska. Security features highlighted include ID TheftSmart™, phishing scam protection, privacy safeguards, HTTPS encryption, Wi-Fi safety guidance, anti-virus recommendations, and smartphone security education. As a regional community bank, Farmers and Merchants serves customers who value local relationships and decision-making over large national institutions. However, the website provides limited information about specific rates, fees, loan terms, or competitive advantages beyond community focus. The bank's product offerings are standard for regional community banks, and without detailed fee schedules or rate information publicly available, customers cannot easily compare costs against larger institutions.
services: Online banking and mobile app access
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Established local institution with 130+ years of family ownership and community commitment
Specialized agricultural lending services tailored to farming operations
Mobile banking app available on both Android and Apple platforms
Free ATM access for account holders
Online check deposit feature available through website and mobile app
Comprehensive security resources including ID theft protection education and phishing scam guidance
cons: Website provides no specific information about interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules for comparison
Limited to Ashland, Nebraska area; no indication of expanded branch network or national reach
No details provided about loan approval requirements, processing times, or lending standards
Mortgage center mentioned but no specific loan products, rates, or refinancing options detailed
No information about credit card rewards, benefits, or competitive advantages over national issuers
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers in Nebraska seeking specialized farm lending
Ashland-area residents and businesses preferring local decision-making over national chain banks
Customers seeking FDIC-insured basic banking products in a community-focused environment
Small business owners needing relationship-based commercial lending
diagnosis: Farmers and Merchants Bank is best for rural Nebraska residents and agricultural businesses seeking FDIC-insured banking with local ownership and personal service. The main caveat is the lack of publicly available rate and fee information, requiring customers to contact the bank directly at 402-944-3316 to compare products against larger regional or national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Family-owned community bank in Ashland, Nebraska serving personal, business, and agricultural customers with FDIC insurance and local decision-making.
fdic_official_name: Farmers and Merchants Bank of Ashland
